title: Browser Trust, CORS, CSRF, Cookies, and Security Headers · Transfer Build
summary: An independent service team receives a different tenant, traffic, trust, persistence, and failure profile and transfers browser trust, cors, csrf, cookies, and security headers into a new browser request and response-sharing policy matrix. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of browser request and response-sharing policy matrix with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Implement an exact origin allowlist, preflight method and field checks, credentials, exposed fields, Vary, cache duration, null origin policy, and failure behavior.
Separate browser response-sharing controls from server authentication, authorization, network clients, and cross-origin request sending.
Protect cookie-authenticated mutations with safe-method semantics, tokens, trusted Origin or Referer, Fetch Metadata, and explicit exceptional-client handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Cors Origin Method Fields
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 6292 in a document service receiving slow compressed uploads. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to keep CORS response sharing distinct from CSRF mutation authorization; reject a replay before any external effect; then remove one trusted proxy hop and recompute authority from the socket boundary. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Implement an exact origin allowlist, preflight method and field checks, credentials, exposed fields, Vary, cache duration, null origin policy, and failure behavior.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Cors Origin Method Fields.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for implement an exact origin allowlist, preflight method and field checks, credentials, exposed fields, vary, cache duration, null origin policy, and failure behavior. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Cors Origin Method Fields, keep Cors Origin Method Fields connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Security Header Policy
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Security Header Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab team handles case 7197 in a document service receiving slow compressed uploads. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to keep CORS response sharing distinct from CSRF mutation authorization; reject a replay before any external effect; then rotate a signing key while an old valid session remains within policy. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Deploy CSP, frame-ancestors, HSTS at the correct authority, MIME sniffing prevention, referrer policy, permissions policy, and cache controls with browser tests.
